PROOF OF SERVICE OF MOTION TO STRIKE FILED BY DEFENDANT AUCTION.COM, LLC (ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS "AUCTION .COM, INC.")

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT AUCTION.COM, LLC'S MOTION TO TRANSFER VENUE IS CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO FEBRUARY 19, 2014. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT AUCTION.COM, LLC'S MOTION TO TRANSFER VENUE IS GRANTED. THE CASE IS TRANSFERRED TO SAN MATEO COUNTY. GOVT. CODE SEC. 12965(B) PROVIDES THAT VENUE IS PROPER IN THE COUNTY WHERE: (1) THE DISCRIMINATORY CONDUCT IS ALLEGED TO HAVE OCCURRED; (2) THE RELEVANT RECORDS ARE MAINTAINED AND ADMINISTERED; OR (3) WHERE THE AGGRIEVED INDIVIDUALS WOULD HAVE WORKED BUT FOR THE ALLEGED DISCRIMINATORY ACTS. IT IS UNDISPUTED THAT UNDER THE FIRST TWO PRONGS VENUE IS NOT PROPER IN SAN FRANCISCO COUNTY. AS TO THE THIRD PRONG, THE FOCUS IS ON WHERE THE EMPLOYEE WOULD HAVE WORKED BUT FOR THE ALLEGED DISCRIMINATION. HERE, IT IS UNDISPUTED THAT (1) DEFENDANT DOES NOT HAVE AN OFFICE IN SAN FRANCISCO COUNTY (DECLARATION OF SHERYL ROLAND, 3); (2) PLAINTIFF WAS HIRED TO WORK IN THE SAN MATEO COUNTY OFFICE (ID., 5); (3) PLAINTIFF WAS TERMINATED OUT OF THE SAN MATEO COUNTY OFFICE (ID., 7); AND (4) DEFENDANT HAD A POLICY REQUIRING ALL EMPLOYEES TO SEEK APPROVAL TO WORK FROM HOME ON A REGULAR BASIS AND PLAINTIFF NEVER SUBMITTED SUCH A REQUEST (ID., 6). THESE FACTS ESTABLISH THAT PLAINTIFF WOULD HAVE WORKED OUT OF THE SAN MATEO COUNTY OFFICE BUT FOR THE ALLEGED DISCRIMINATORY ACTS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR5976

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-02-2014 Off Calendar

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-21-2014 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Venue
Off Calendar Feb-19-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT AUCTION.COM, LLC'S Motion To Transfer Venue is granted. The case is transferred to San Mateo County. Govt. Code sec. 12965(b) provides that venue is proper in the county where: (1) the discriminatory conduct is alleged to have occurred; (2) the relevant records are maintained and administered; or (3) where the aggrieved individuals would have worked but for the alleged discriminatory acts. It is undisputed that under the first two prongs venue is not proper in San Francisco County. As to the third prong, the focus is on where the employee would have worked but for the alleged discrimination. Here, it is undisputed that (1) Defendant does not have an office in San Francisco County (Declaration of Sheryl Roland, 3); (2) Plaintiff was hired to work in the San Mateo County office (Id., 5); (3) Plaintiff was terminated out of the San Mateo County office (Id., 7); and (4) Defendant had a policy requiring all employees to seek approval to work from home on a regular basis and Plaintiff never submitted such a request (Id., 6). These facts establish that Plaintiff would have worked out of the San Mateo County office but for the alleged discriminatory acts. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR5976

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Venue
